requirements.title: Rule Update Protocol
requirements.description: Define the LLM-governed process for updating local rules from maestro-flow.

requirements.title: Command Rule Set
requirements.description: Create local command facts, scenario types, workflow paths, and recommendation rules with sourceRefs.

requirements.title: Stepwise Canvas Model
requirements.description: Replace fixed single-flow model with generated nodes, edges, recommendation groups, and branch-aware state.

requirements.title: Canvas Interaction UI
requirements.description: Redesign UI around a large canvas, initial scenario node, node-local choices, and lightweight evidence surfaces.

requirements.title: Route Persistence
requirements.description: Persist recent routes and favorites with recommendation evidence in browser storage.

requirements.title: Route Sharing and Gallery
requirements.description: Generate standalone share URLs, optionally publish routes to a public gallery, and manage/revoke shared content.

constraints.constraint: The app MUST introduce a local CommandRuleSet layer consumed by React.
constraints.rationale: Current ScenarioModel is one authored flow.

constraints.constraint: Rule provenance MUST cite maestro-flow submodule paths.
Equal-rank recommendations MUST be shown as peer options, not forced into a fake primary command.
constraints.rationale: Avoid misleading users when rules are ambiguous.

constraints.constraint: Saved routes MUST include schemaVersion, ruleVersion, name, scenario, command nodes, recommendation evidence, and sourceRefs.
constraints.rationale: Saved routes must remain understandable after UI/rule changes.

constraints.constraint: Shared route records MUST distinguish private share links, public gallery visibility, and revoked links.
constraints.rationale: Users requested separate link sharing, gallery publishing, gallery unpublishing, and link revocation.

constraints.constraint: Edges SHOULD support user-authored notes explaining why a command path was chosen.
constraints.rationale: Route readers need rationale on connections, not only node-level command evidence.

domain.problem_statement: Design a canvas-based Maestro command recommender that uses source-backed local rules from maestro-flow and supports stepwise, multi-branch route generation.

terminology.definition: Local data layer containing command facts, scenario types, workflow paths, and recommendation rules.
A ranked or equal-rank set of command candidates shown on a node.
Browser-persisted route with recommendation evidence and version metadata.
Route map published to a standalone URL with optional gallery visibility.
User-authored note on a connection explaining why a choice was made.

domain.audience: Users learning which Maestro command or workflow path to choose

non_goals.title: Real project state parsing in MVP
Browser runtime LLM recommendation
non_goals.rationale: LLM assists updates, not runtime recommendation.

non_goals.title: Public gallery in static-only MVP
non_goals.rationale: Gallery requires online storage/API, moderation, and revocation semantics.

insights.summary: Split rule data from canvas state to keep recommendation logic testable outside React.
Stepwise branch growth should use node-local recommendation cards and compact evidence disclosure.
The product promise is understandable command selection, not a static documentation sidebar.
Public gallery sharing is a distinct online capability beyond browser-local route persistence.

open_questions.question: Tree, swimlane, or auto-layout graph for parallel branches?
Should generated rules live in JSON or TypeScript?
What backend/storage should host shared routes and gallery entries?
What moderation or abuse controls are required for public gallery submissions?
